name: InsydeH2O / American Megatrends (AMI) UEFI CRTM family
description: InsydeH2O (Insyde Corp.) and AMI Aptio are the two most common third-party UEFI firmware stacks shipped by OEMs (Framework, Acer, Asus, Gigabyte, MSI, HP consumer, many others). They write EV_S_CRTM_VERSION as UTF-16LE strings identifying the CRTM build.

platforms: Legacy enterprise laptops — rarely seen on modern hardware

notes: Third-party firmware vendors don't typically ship their own TPM CA — the EK chain goes through the TPM chip manufacturer's CA (Infineon / STMicro / Nuvoton / AMD / Intel). Use `manufacturers.json' to resolve the EK vendor, not this file.
source: CRTM version strings observed across Linux hosts with `tpm2_pcrread + /sys/kernel/security/tpm0/binary_bios_measurements'.
